purpose: Implement story-scoped changes on dedicated branches using the approved contract, tests, and knowledge base context.
inputs: Approved and gate-passed core and UI implementation evidence
Existing plugin source tree and test harness as mutable implementation targets, not approval context
outputs: observe CLI command, installed-plugin launch skill, and packaged runtime integration
Installed-artifact smoke tests, browser QA evidence, and user documentation
Optional trace narrative support for pre-generated Codex explanations with explicit legacy fallback
Regression fixes for custom output routing, self-mutating story context, root-swap safety, and private-reasoning redaction
validation: decision and assumption changes are recorded
no unauthorized tool or resource use
npm test and npm run check pass; npm pack includes all observatory and skill assets.
An isolated tarball smoke test launches from installed package paths and serves authenticated health, model, source, and UI successfully.
Desktop and 360px browser QA against the real loopback server pass with no material accessibility or fidelity gap.
Existing commands retain compatible behavior and help documents observe and narrative options.

notes: Preserve the dependency-free Node.js runtime and browser-native ES modules.
Use browser QA only against the local loopback observatory.

contextualization.summary: Integrate the completed Change Observatory core and UI into the published plugin so an installed copy can launch it through the packaged CLI or bundled Codex skill. Inputs are limited to immutable requirement and completed lane evidence; the mutable project baseline is refreshed only after integration.

context_sources.excerpt: id REQ-CHANGE-OBSERVATORY-001 kind requirement schema_version requirement v1 title Install-integrated SDLC Change Observatory summary Provide a local visual application shipped inside the Agentic SDLC plugin that reconstructs what was requested decided changed verified and released across stories phases contracts and iterations for technical and non-technical readers. status proposed acceptance_criteria After plugin installation agentic-sdlc observe --root <project> launches the included application without a separate build or cloud service. The overview answers What was asked? What changed? and Why was it decided? from canonical .sdlc evidence and links every claim to source records. The timeline shows story iterations across Discovery Analysis Design Implementation Validation and Release distinguishing recorded inferred missing blocked and complete states. Contracts approvals decisions assumptions risks changed files tests gates handoffs sync events and release evidence are navigable with raw-record access. Natural-language explanations are clearly labeled as generated contain no private chain-of-thought and expose only stored rationale inputs outputs alternatives and evidence. T

# Change delivery evidence ## What was asked? Build the local dependency-free core of the install-integrated SDLC Change Observatory normalize canonical `.sdlc` evidence into a stable lineage model and expose it through a safe read-only loopback API. The originating requirement is REQ-CHANGE-OBSERVATORY-001 (../../../requirements/REQ-CHANGE-OBSERVATORY-001.json); the story boundary is ST-OBSERVATORY-CORE (../story.json). ## Scope and non-goals Delivered in this lane - versioned normalization of canonical `.sdlc` records; - explicit `recorded` `inferred` `missing` and `malformed` provenance; - bounded diagnostics for empty partial legacy malformed and oversized evidence; - read-only HTTP endpoints for health normalized lineage and source records; - static-asset serving primitives for the bundled UI; - loopback-only binding Host validation restrictive CSP no CORS and path containment; - deterministic unit and security tests. Excluded by contract and left to the integration/UI lanes CLI dispatch browser launch package wiring visual components external services deployment dependencies and any reconstruction of unstored history. Private chain-of-thought is neither generated nor exposed;

# Change delivery evidence ## What was asked? Deliver the accessible visual lineage experience for Change Observatory inside the Agentic SDLC plugin. The primary screen must explain what was requested what changed and why; expose iteration and phase lineage contracts decisions changes verification explainability and raw records; and remain useful from 360px to desktop without a framework or build step. Canonical request REQ-CHANGE-OBSERVATORY-001 (../../../requirements/REQ-CHANGE-OBSERVATORY-001.json). Story ST-OBSERVATORY-UI (../story.json). The original execution contract (../../../contracts/contract-ST-OBSERVATORY-UI-implementation.json) and freshness-safe v2 contract (../../../contracts/contract-ST-OBSERVATORY-UI-implementation-v2.json) are retained in lineage; contract-ST-OBSERVATORY-UI-implementation-v3 (../../../contracts/contract-ST-OBSERVATORY-UI-implementation-v3.json) preserves the exact scope and records this UI evidence as a delta of the core implementation evidence. ## Scope and non-goals Delivered scope - Build-free browser-native HTML CSS and modular JavaScript under `ui/change-observatory/`. - Versioned `GET /api/v1/observatory` client and same-origin canonical raw

questions.question: What is the install-time user path?
questions.answer: From a package install, run agentic-sdlc observe --root <project>. From an installed Codex plugin, invoke the bundled change-observatory skill, which resolves the plugin-local CLI without modifying PATH.

questions.question: How is package completeness proven?
questions.answer: Pack the artifact, install it in an isolated temporary prefix, launch the installed CLI, query health/data/assets, verify security boundaries, and terminate cleanly.

questions.question: What reasoning may be recorded?
questions.answer: Only shareable rationale summaries, inputs, outputs, alternatives, and clearly labeled explanations derived from recorded evidence; never private chain-of-thought.

constraints: Bundle every required runtime, UI, and skill asset through package.json files; no postinstall or generated frontend build.
Support --host only as loopback, --port with 0 for an ephemeral port, --no-open for automation, graceful shutdown, and a per-run local access token for evidence APIs.
Do not change unrelated CLI behavior or require secrets, cloud access, deployment, global PATH mutation, or external package installation.
assumptions: Opening a browser uses a cross-platform best-effort built-in OS command and failure to open does not terminate the server.

approvals.summary: Approved under Antonio's delegated Change Observatory scope. V3 keeps the same install-ready product boundary, excludes the mutable baseline from immutable context, and includes local API authorization plus fail-closed evidence handling found necessary by security review.

scope.principle: An automation approval is valid only under an explicit delegated approval level or configured automation policy recorded in the summary or evidence.
scope.approval_level: Complete the already approved Change Observatory integration under contract-ST-OBSERVATORY-INTEGRATION-implementation-v3 and confirm ST-OBSERVATORY-INTEGRATION task start, including install-ready launcher, plugin skill, safe narrative lineage, security/regression fixes, package tests, documentation, baseline refresh, and local browser QA; no deployment, public release, secrets, destructive actions, or unrelated work.
